changes_made: Changed 'k8s_rbac_role_binding_restriction' to 'k8s_rbac_role_binding_least_privilege' for clarity and consistency
review_notes: The functions accurately map to Kubernetes RBAC controls related to access enforcement and privilege management. The naming conventions mostly follow the prescribed pattern, but 'k8s_rbac_role_binding_restriction' was adjusted to better reflect the principle of least privilege, which is more descriptive and aligns with the control's intent.
naming_improvements: Use 'least_privilege' instead of 'restriction' to clearly indicate the principle being enforced
title: Access Enforcement - Granting its privileges to other subjects;
step1_reasoning: The control AC-3(2) focuses on access enforcement and ensuring that privileges are not inappropriately granted to other subjects. In Kubernetes, this maps to RBAC configurations where roles and privileges should be carefully managed to prevent unauthorized privilege escalation. The functions focus on enforcing least privilege, avoiding cluster-wide admin bindings, and ensuring role bindings are appropriately restricted.
